introduction:
supertool batches multiple file operations into one Bash call.

Invoke via Bash:
./supertool \
    'read:supertool.py' \
    'read:tests/test_ops.py' \
    'grep:def op_:supertool.py:20' \
    'map:supertool.py'

Rules:
- One call, many ops. 6-7 per call is normal. Each separate call wastes money.
- Paths are relative to project root.
- Single quotes around each op.
- Use Read tool ONLY for the file you're about to Edit — supertool for everything else.
output-format:
Each operation returns a header followed by its output:

```
--- read:supertool.py ---
(979 lines, 35000 bytes)
     1→#!/usr/bin/env python3
     2→
     3→import os
     4→import sys

--- grep:def op_:supertool.py:5 ---
(3 results, limit 5)
supertool.py
  362:def op_read  [362]
  367:def op_grep  [367]
  464:def op_around  [464]

--- map:supertool.py ---
(1 files, tier: tree-sitter)
supertool.py (979 lines)
  def op_read  [362]
  def op_grep  [367]
  def op_around  [464]
```

read-grep.description: Inline filter — only matching lines, original line numbers preserved

grep.description: Search (10 results default). CONTEXT shows N surrounding lines before/after each match

grep-count.description: Count mode — match counts per file, no content

around.description: First match in a single file + N lines context (default 10). One-shot grep+read

glob.description: Find files (supports **). Auto-reads if concrete path (no wildcards)

map.description: Symbol tree (classes, functions, methods, constants). Three-tier: tree-sitter > ctags > regex

stat.description: File/directory metadata: size, last modified, type

around_line.description: Show N lines of context around a specific line number (default 10). Target line marked with →

between.description: Return a chunk of a file. Symbol mode: full body of a named function/method/class via tree-sitter (any language tree-sitter parses). Pattern mode (re: prefix): inclusive line slice from first line matching START regex to first line after matching END regex.

tree.description: Directory structure with depth limit (default 3). Hides dotfiles.

replace_dry.description: Preview replacements without modifying files. Shows diff-style output (- old / + new) per occurrence.

replace.description: Find and replace OLD with NEW across all files in PATH. Returns receipt with files modified and count per file.
